rulings.Case Title: LEE SUMMERLOTT VS. CURTIS BELTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-562429 WITH CASE CGC-16-549855
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 8, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT CURTIS BELTON's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-562429 WITH CASE CGC-16-549855 Defendant's motion to consolidate is GRANTED per the parties' stipulation. CGC-17-562429 is consolidated with CGC-16-549855 for all purposes. The court designates CGC-16-549855 the lead case number and all future filings shall be in that case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO PROBATE DIVISION ** RONALD DILLER VS. THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Administrative Motion To Seal Portions Of Reply Memorandum In Support Of Motion To Transfer Case To Probate Department Of San Mateo Superior Court And Supporting Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 8, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION, THE HELEN DILLER FOUNDATION, JACLYN SAFIER, INDIVIDUALLY, AS TRUSTEE OF THE Administrative Motion To Seal Portions Of Reply Memorandum In Support Of Motion To Transfer Case To Probate Department Of San Mateo Superior Court And Supporting Documents Defendant Jaclyn Safier's motion to seal portions of Defendant's reply memorandum is DENIED. Defendants have not satisfied their burden of showing substantial probability of prejudice if the sealing is not ordered under NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Superior Court (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178. General allegations of settlors' intent and potential interference with charitable operations is not sufficient to overcome the public right to access this information. (See Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1283-84 (denying sealing despite contractual obligation to avoid disclosing settlement agreement because the defendant did not show substantial prejudice to its business interests).) Moreover, the parts of the trust agreements sought to be sealed are not narrowly tailored and the redactions in the motions contain nonsensitive information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO PROBATE DIVISION ** RONALD DILLER VS. THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Case To Probate Department Of San Mateo Superior Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 8, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANTs THE HELEN DILLER FAMILY FOUNDATION, THE HELEN DILLER FOUNDATION, and JACLYN SAFIER, INDIVIDUALLY, AS TRUSTEE OF THE Motion To Transfer Case To Probate Department Of San Mateo Superior Court Defendant Jaclyn Safier's motion to transfer case to probate department of San Mateo Superior Court is GRANTED. Plaintiff Ronald Diller's complaint seeks to invalidate certain trust provisions, question the validity of a trust amendment, and challenge whether certain trust provisions comport with the settlors' intentions. Therefore, the complaint implicates the internal affairs of the trusts as described in Probate Code section 17200 such that San Mateo Superior Court's probate department has jurisdiction over these claims. Because Plaintiff is a beneficiary of the trust, he has access to probate proceedings and he is not deprived of remedies sought by his causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb
